channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Loads of ivyvon pavement. Bloxking path, 24th April
item.description:
A house has letbloads of ibyvfall into the footpath
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sundridge Avenue, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin Bags Left in front of Tesco, 24th April
item.description:
Somebody constantly leaves bin bags in front of Tesco. Foxes come at night and spread the rubbish. These people need to be fined otherwise they will continue. There’s only so much people can do if there is no support and enforcement from the council.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bounds Green Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish constantly thrown on the ground or overflowing, 24th April
item.description:
This bin is constantly overflowing. Residents from the flats don’t seem to care and trash ends up on the ground, spread by foxes and ending up in the New River. The council needs to provide more facilities to dispose of rubbish and fine the residents if rubbish is not properly disposed of.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Whittington Road, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Metal pole and clothes airer, 24th April
item.description:
Please collect

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queen's Mead Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Gravel covering public footpath, 24th April
item.description:
The area of public footpath outside 176 Rydal Drive is covered with loose gravel making it difficult and dangerous for pedestrians to walk safely. Please can this be dealt with with asap before someone has an accident.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 172, Rydal Drive, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Massive potholes near 1 marshall grove, 24th April
item.description:
We are experiencing a serious issue due to large potholes on the road near our house. These potholes are getting bigger each day. When heavy vehicles such as lorries and buses pass over them, the impact causes strong vibrations that make our house shake noticeably. This has become very disturbing due to the constant noise and shaking. More importantly, we are concerned that these repeated vibrations are beginning to cause cracks in our property. We kindly request that this issue is inspected and repaired as a matter of urgency, before it leads to further damage or safety risks for the residents of the street.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Marshalls Grove, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Please urgently fix the deep potholes on this section of road., 24th April
item.description:
You must fix the deep potholes on the A406 junction as I nearly got thrown off one when I hit one this evening in the dark as I headed north on Conley Hatch Lane. I had full lights and did not see it. There will be a nasty accident here and you will be liable. Immediate attention please.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pinkham Way, Colney Hatch, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned bike, 24th April
item.description:
Bike missing all its wheels and parts etc. hanging near school entrance and dangerous. Please remove

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lordship Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Brambles growing over from house on merlin rd, 24th April
item.description:
Dangerous for small children Will soon obstruct alleyway

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Danson Lane, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Norwood Road SE27 9HS outside number 489 to 491, 24th April
item.description:
Norwood Road SE27 9HS outside number 489 to 491 (KFC shop) is a Lambeth food waste bin which has a powered coated surface and a combined litter and recycling bin which seem to have a some kind of plastic coating. Both graffiti filth tagged. Please forward fixmystreet.com link to your contractor for reporting pin &, picture.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 493, Norwood Road, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on reverse of sign, 24th April
item.description:
These are tags

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 144, Kennington Park Road, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: On pavement, corner frognal with oak hill park, 24th April
item.description:
Bike dumped next to Forest bike. Blocking narrow pavement in a location where cars go round the corner on the wrong side of the road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Oak Hill Park, West Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Untaxed van, 24th April
item.description:
Again another untaxed van from the man at no 286 This one has been here for 4 days now. He has constantly got 5-6 plus vehicles mostly untaxed sitting along this stretch of road which is hard to park on at the best of times. You guys really need to start sending clampers out regularly around here

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 276, High Street, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Melbourne Grove (End of the road near zebra crossing as if walking towards Grove Vale direction)-Flytipping, 24th April
item.description:
Large plastic sheet on pavement. Needs to be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4, Melbourne Grove, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Corner frognal/oak hill park., 24th April
item.description:
On narrow pavement, has been there for at least a week.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 71, Frognal, South Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mechanics being carried out on the street, 24th April
item.description:
Over the last couple of weeks a man living on Cattistock Road has been carrying out repairs on multiple vehicles on the parking to the left hand side of the Cattistock junction where it meets Beaconsfield Road. The vehicles are actually parked on Beaconsfield Road outside 263,265 and 267 mainly. There has been a red van, blue car and green car which have been parked, had the tyres and brakes removed and engine repairs carried out. The vehicle then leaves a new one arrives in its place. Work is being carried out early around 6:30am and late at night - later than 9pm - this is not only causing a noise issue but also a safety issue with repeated banging and drilling, the vehicles are being propped up on boxes and bricks with nothing protecting the public. This is also taking up valuable parking spaces which residents of the road need. If this man is legally running this business, they should be running it outside their own home and using their own parking space, not everyone else’s. There are strong evidence to suggest these are not personal vehicles and are being repaired for money. There is no work carried out usually during the day, it is all being done early morning and late evenings so I am assuming they are doing this around their actual job.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beaconsfield Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parking space being ‘reserved’ by bins, 24th April
item.description:
The m people at 91 Longhill Road are ‘reserving’ a parking space outside their property by putting their bin in the road. This is causing an obstruction and I believe may be illegal under the Highways Act 1980.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Longhill Road, Catford South, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 48c zmelbourne Grove-Flytipping, 24th April
item.description:
A television dish has been left on the pavement, causing potential obstruction. Also not appropriate disposal of an electrical item.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 48c, Melbourne Grove, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
